gloss: for (plural) S to walk, go (by walking or as a general term) to P

examples.tlingit: Chʼu tle áyá a náḵ neil oo.aatch yá ḵaa jáag̱adi.
examples.english: They would even go home from their kill.

gloss: for (plural) S to walk, go (by walking or as a general term) down along P

examples.tlingit: Chʼu tle áyá a náḵ neil oo.aatch yá ḵaa jáag̱adi.
examples.english: They would even go home from their kill.

examples.tlingit: Chʼu tle áyá a náḵ neil oo.aatch yá ḵaa jáag̱adi.
examples.english: They would even go home from their kill.

gloss: for S to feed O to N; for S to give O to N to eat
for S to eat O
for S to eat
for O to miss, be lonesome for, wish to see N

examples.tlingit: Yá sítʼ tayeenáx̱ has wulhaashí áyá

examples.tlingit: Chʼu tle áyá a náḵ neil oo.aatch yá ḵaa jáag̱adi.
examples.english: They would even go home from their kill.

gloss: for (plural) O to jump (see ji-k'ein for singular)
for S to give O an opportunity to speak; for S to give O directions as to what to say
for S to say (a certain thing); for S to confess, acknowledge, declare (a certain thing)
for S to tell, say (that) to O; for S to ask O to do (that)
for S to give O orders, instructions (esp. concerning work)
for S to give orders to, command, instruct O
for S to take, lead (plural) O by the hand

examples.tlingit: Chʼu tle áyá a náḵ neil oo.aatch yá ḵaa jáag̱adi.

gloss: for S to carry, take (plural) O (esp. baggage) to P

examples.tlingit: Chʼu tle áyá a náḵ neil oo.aatch yá ḵaa jáag̱adi.
examples.english: They would even go home from their kill.

gloss: for S to give, take, hand O (small, round or hoop-like objects) to N

examples.tlingit: Chʼu tle áyá a náḵ neil oo.aatch yá ḵaa jáag̱adi.

gloss: for the tide to rise, come in
for O to move in, come around, crowd at P (of large numbers, esp. people, birds)
for the time to come for P
for S to squat, sit down low; for S to sit down quickly, squat down; for S to land (of waterfowl, plane) (singular subject)
for (plural) S to sit, sit down (esp. act of sitting)
for (singular) S to sit, sit down (esp. act of sitting)

examples.source: The Woman Who Married the Bear

examples.tlingit: Chʼu tle du eet ÿaan wuhaaÿí, chʼa wáa sá shukanduwajáa, chʼa a yáx̱ ḵoowanook.
examples.english: So then when he got hungry, just how she had instructed him, that was how he acted.

gloss: for (singular) S to stand up, rise

examples.tlingit: tléináx̱ áyú yú éeg̱i yan aa uhaanch.
examples.english: One would stand out on the beach.

examples.tlingit: Násʼgi aa áwé ḵu.aa, áa yax̱ haan áwé Kaaḵáakw shakée;
examples.english: The third one would stand at the arch of Kaaḵáakw;

gloss: for (singular) S to keep standing; for (singular) S to stand firm

examples.english: The third one would stand at the arch of Kaaḵáakw;

gloss: for (plural) S to stand up, rise
for S to drink O in sips, sip O (esp. hot liquids)

for (singular) O to fall asleep (literally: for S (sleep) to kill (singular) O)
for (plural) O to fall asleep (literally: for S (sleep) to kill (plural) O)
for S to know, learn how to do O

gloss: for S to know, be acquainted with, make known O (esp. people, facts); for S to learn O (esp. facts)

examples.english: in front of Spring Water Point

gloss: for S to want O (a certain way); for S to be decided about O; for S to agree to O
for O to want to do, feel like doing (that); for O to feel a certain way
for S to love O
